title: calceolaria ascendens subsp. pristiphylla
intro: Calceolaria ascendens subsp. pristiphylla, also known as the 'slipper flower', is a flowering plant species native to the Andean region of South America. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Calceolaria ascendens subsp. pristiphylla is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
